domain_title: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions

questions.stem: A team fine-tuned a model on proprietary data and needs a dedicated endpoint with predictable throughput. Which deployment type is required?

options.text: Serverless API
Managed Compute
Global Batch
Model Router

questions.explanation: Serverless API deployments cannot host custom fine-tuned weights. Managed Compute runs the model on a dedicated container endpoint that hosts custom weights and delivers consistent throughput through Azure Machine Learning scaling rules.

questions.stem: Which statements about inference SDK selection in Azure AI Foundry are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The azure-ai-inference package is the recommended cross-model abstraction over the Model Inference REST API
Switching models with azure-ai-inference can require only changing the model parameter
azure-ai-projects is needed for Foundry-native features such as agents and evaluations
Managed compute autoscaling is configured directly in the Foundry portal deployment blade

questions.explanation: The azure-ai-inference package provides a model-agnostic interface, so only the model parameter changes when switching, while azure-ai-projects unlocks Foundry-native features. Managed compute autoscaling is configured through Azure Machine Learning online endpoint rules, not the Foundry portal.

questions.stem: A RAG application sometimes returns facts that appear in no retrieved document. Which evaluator best measures this problem?

options.text: Fluency
Relevance
Groundedness
Coherence

questions.explanation: Groundedness measures whether each claim in a response is supported by the provided context, so a low score flags fabrication. Relevance measures topical fit, while Fluency and Coherence measure language quality and logical flow.

questions.stem: For the Northwind Health assistant, an Azure AI Search index must hold embeddings for semantic similarity. What field type must the vector field use?

options.text: Edm.String
Collection(Edm.Single)
Edm.Double
Collection(Edm.String)

questions.explanation: Vector fields in Azure AI Search must be declared as Collection(Edm.Single) to hold the array of single-precision floats that make up an embedding. String or scalar types cannot store a vector.

questions.stem: A team built an orchestration pipeline using Prompt flow. What is the recommended migration target for new development?

options.text: The legacy Completions API
The Microsoft Agent Framework and Responses API
A hand-rolled polling loop over raw REST
The Assistants API only

questions.explanation: Prompt flow is deprecated, and the Microsoft Agent Framework together with the Responses API is its designated replacement for Foundry workflow orchestration. The Completions API is not an orchestration surface.

questions.stem: Prompt flow is deprecated, and new Foundry workflow development should favor the Agent Framework and Responses API rather than Prompt flow.
questions.explanation: Prompt flow is officially deprecated with a scheduled retirement, so production best practice for new workflows is to build on the Agent Framework and the Responses API.

questions.stem: A compliance team must verify that no generated content contains self-harm language. Which evaluator category applies, and at what granularity does it operate?

options.text: A quality evaluator operating on full conversations
A safety evaluator operating on individual turns
An agent evaluator operating on tool calls
A textual similarity evaluator operating on references

questions.explanation: Safety evaluators detect harm categories such as self-harm and operate on individual turns rather than entire conversation threads. Quality and agent evaluators measure different dimensions and are not the correct category for content-safety validation.

questions.stem: Which statements about the Foundry Evaluation service are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: AI-assisted quality evaluators require a deployed judge model
Safety evaluators can score a full multi-turn conversation as a single unit
Evaluations can target datasets, model turns, agent runs, or historical traces
Agent evaluators such as Tool Call Success need agent trace data, not just model outputs

questions.explanation: Quality evaluators use a judge model, evaluations can target datasets, models, agents, or traces, and agent evaluators need trace data. Safety evaluators operate on individual turns rather than scoring an entire conversation as one unit.

questions.stem: A developer needs one authenticated session that can both list a Foundry project's connections and obtain an OpenAI-compatible inference client. Which class provides both?

options.text: AzureKeyCredential
AIProjectClient from azure-ai-projects
SearchClient from azure-search-documents
SpeechConfig from the Speech SDK

questions.explanation: AIProjectClient wraps the Foundry project endpoint and exposes sub-clients for connections and inference in a single authenticated session. The other classes are scoped to credentials, search, or speech only.

questions.stem: An agent tool references an external Azure AI Search service, but the deployment fails to reach it. What is a common root cause specific to Foundry?

options.text: The model context window is too small
No project connection is configured for the external service
Streaming was disabled on the client
The judge model was not deployed

questions.explanation: Foundry agents reach external services through configured project connections. A missing connection is a frequent deployment failure because the tool cannot authenticate or query the service without one.

questions.stem: Which statements about connecting an application to a Foundry project are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: DefaultAzureCredential selects the right credential across local, CI, and Azure-hosted environments without code changes
Foundry User is the least-privilege role for inference and agent invocation
Full prompt and response content is captured in traces by default
The project endpoint follows the pattern .../api/projects/<project-name>

questions.explanation: DefaultAzureCredential chains through providers automatically, Foundry User is the least-privilege inference role, and the project endpoint uses the .../api/projects/ path. Content recording is disabled by default because prompt and response text may contain PII.

questions.stem: What is the minimum RBAC role a developer needs to invoke an agent in a Foundry project?

options.text: Foundry Owner
Foundry Account Owner
Foundry User
Contributor at subscription scope

questions.explanation: Foundry User is the least-privilege role that grants inference and agent invocation rights. The Owner and Account Owner roles are for administration and should not be assigned to application developers by default.

questions.stem: Which elements make up the definition of a Foundry agent? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: A model selected from the catalog
Instructions defining role, scope, and constraints
A set of tool definitions
A dedicated Azure subscription per agent

questions.explanation: An agent is defined by a model, its instructions, and its tools. Agents do not require a dedicated subscription; they run within a Foundry project.

questions.stem: A developer building a multi-turn chatbot does not want to manually resend the full conversation history on each user message. Which Foundry construct handles this automatically?

options.text: A thread
A run step
A guardrail
A scoring profile

questions.explanation: A thread stores the ordered message history across turns, so appending a new message and starting a run is enough. The Foundry service supplies the full thread context to the model on each run.

questions.stem: An agent run enters requires_action, and the application submits tool outputs 15 minutes after the run started. What happens?

options.text: The outputs are accepted and the run completes
The run fails because tool outputs expire 10 minutes after run creation
The run restarts automatically from the beginning
The outputs queue until the next run

questions.explanation: Tool outputs must be submitted within 10 minutes of run creation. A submission after that window is rejected and the run transitions to a failed state.

questions.stem: An agent must answer questions from a fixed set of PDF documents without the developer writing any retrieval code. Which built-in tool fits?

options.text: Function calling
File search
Code Interpreter
Grounding with Bing Search

questions.explanation: File search automatically indexes uploaded documents and lets the agent retrieve and cite content, requiring no custom retrieval code. Function calling suits dynamic data, and Bing grounding targets live web content.

questions.stem: An agent is configured with an Azure AI Search tool and a valid connection but returns no results despite data being present in the index. What should be verified?

options.text: That the model temperature is above 0.5
That the Foundry project connection name matches the index name exactly
That Code Interpreter is also attached
That streaming is enabled on the client

questions.explanation: The Azure AI Search tool requires the project connection name to match the index name. A mismatch causes silent retrieval failures where the tool appears attached but returns nothing.

questions.stem: A team wants an agent to call an internal REST API described by an OpenAPI 3.0 spec, authenticating with the agent's managed identity. Which tool supports this?

options.text: The Code Interpreter tool
The OpenAPI tool with managed identity authentication
The File Search tool
The Fabric Data Agent

questions.explanation: The OpenAPI tool accepts a 3.0 or 3.1 spec and supports anonymous, API key, and managed identity authentication, letting the agent call external HTTP services securely without stored credentials.

questions.stem: Which statements about the Azure AI Search tool for Foundry agents are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Each tool instance supports only one index
It requires a configured Foundry project connection
It supports simple, semantic, vector, and hybrid search types
It can query multiple indexes in a single tool instance

questions.explanation: The Azure AI Search tool binds to one index per instance, requires a project connection, and supports several search types. Querying multiple indexes requires the connected agents pattern with a separate tool per index.

questions.stem: A connected-agents design nests an orchestrator, a subagent, and that subagent's own subagent. When the middle subagent tries to call its child, what happens?

options.text: The run completes normally with three tiers
The run fails with an Assistant Tool Call Depth Error
The child subagent is silently ignored
The orchestrator restarts the run

questions.explanation: The connected agents pattern enforces a maximum depth of 2. A subagent cannot invoke its own subagent, and attempting a third tier produces an Assistant Tool Call Depth Error.

questions.stem: For the Contoso Travel concierge, a ConnectedAgentTool is created with name set to "flight price bot". What is the result?

options.text: It works because names are free-form
A validation error, because the name may contain only letters and underscores
The spaces are automatically converted to hyphens
The subagent runs but cannot return results

questions.explanation: The name field for a ConnectedAgentTool must contain only letters and underscores. A value with spaces causes a validation error; flight_price_bot would be valid.

questions.stem: In a connected-agents solution, subagent responses are returned only to the main agent, which synthesizes the final answer for the user.
questions.explanation: Subagents report back to the main orchestrating agent, which combines their outputs into a single cohesive response. End users never interact directly with a subagent.

questions.stem: The Contoso Travel agent has a tool that emails booking confirmations to customers, and the team wants a human to approve every send. Which tool approval mode should be set?

options.text: never_require
always_require
annotate_only
spotlight

questions.explanation: always_require sends an approval request for every invocation through the human-in-the-loop flow, which is appropriate for outbound, potentially irreversible actions such as customer emails. never_require runs fully autonomously.

questions.stem: An agent keeps invoking the wrong tool for certain requests even though the correct tool exists. Which preview guardrail signal is designed to surface this deviation?

options.text: Spotlighting
Task Adherence
Groundedness Pro
Protected Material detection

questions.explanation: Task Adherence detects misaligned tool invocations, including wrong tool selection, incorrect inputs, and responses inconsistent with user intent, producing a signal to block or escalate. Spotlighting mitigates prompt injection instead.

questions.stem: Which statements about safeguards for autonomous Foundry agents are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The AG-UI protocol implements human-in-the-loop approval flows
Spotlighting is a technique to mitigate cross-prompt injection attacks
If no guardrail is explicitly assigned to an agent, the model's default guardrail applies
never_require is the correct mode for irreversible high-risk actions

questions.explanation: AG-UI implements HITL approval, Spotlighting mitigates cross-prompt injection, and the model's default guardrail applies when none is assigned. Irreversible high-risk actions should use always_require, not never_require.

questions.stem: The Foundry Agent Monitoring Dashboard requires an Application Insights resource to be connected to the project before it can display run telemetry.
questions.explanation: Agent run traces stream to Application Insights, and the monitoring dashboard depends on that connection. Without it, no telemetry is captured and the dashboard has nothing to display.

questions.stem: An agent run has a status of failed. What is the first programmatic step to diagnose the root cause?

options.text: Raise the model temperature and re-run
Inspect run.last_error.message
Delete the thread and start over
Increase the PTU allocation

questions.explanation: The last_error property on a failed run holds the error code and message describing why the run failed. After reading it, trace spans in Application Insights pinpoint the specific tool or model step that failed.

questions.stem: A developer sets temperature to 0.9 and top_p to 0.5 at the same time. Why is this discouraged?

options.text: top_p must always be higher than temperature
Temperature and top_p are alternative randomness controls that should not be tuned simultaneously
The model ignores both parameters when set together
Setting both forces deterministic output

questions.explanation: Temperature and top_p both restrict the token distribution independently, so adjusting both at once produces compounding, unpredictable effects. Best practice is to tune one and leave the other at its default.

questions.stem: An application requires the model to always return output conforming to a specific JSON structure. Which response_format setting enforces schema compliance?

options.text: response_format of type text
response_format of type json_object
response_format of type json_schema
No response_format, relying on prompt wording alone

questions.explanation: A json_schema response format validates the output against a defined schema, guaranteeing the exact shape. The json_object type only guarantees syntactically valid JSON, not a specific structure.

questions.stem: Which statements about tuning generation behavior are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: The system message is the most impactful lever and should be tuned before numeric parameters
Lower temperature produces more deterministic responses
max_tokens sets a hard upper limit on response length
temperature and top_p should always be adjusted together for best results

questions.explanation: The system message shapes output most strongly, lower temperature increases determinism, and max_tokens caps response length. Temperature and top_p are alternative controls and should not be adjusted simultaneously.

questions.stem: A team wants a model to catch its own arithmetic errors before returning an answer. Which pattern implements this?

options.text: A single call with a higher temperature
A self-critique loop that generates, critiques, then revises
Disabling the system message
Reducing max_tokens to force brevity

questions.explanation: A self-critique loop makes an initial call, a second call to identify errors, and optionally a third to produce the revised answer, catching mistakes before the user sees the response. The other options do not add a review step.

questions.stem: An application must solve advanced mathematical proofs that require deep multi-step reasoning. Which choice is most reliable?

options.text: A standard chat model with a short prompt
An o-series reasoning model such as o3
An embedding model
A small language model for edge devices

questions.explanation: o-series reasoning models perform internal multi-step reasoning without explicit chain-of-thought prompting and are optimized for math, logic, and complex inference. Standard, embedding, and small models are less reliable for advanced proofs.

questions.stem: An operations team needs full prompt and response text captured in distributed traces for debugging. What must they change?

options.text: Increase the model context window
Set AZURE_TRACING_GEN_AI_CONTENT_RECORDING_ENABLED to true
Switch to a Serverless API deployment
Assign the Foundry Account Owner role

questions.explanation: By default traces capture span metadata but not content text, to avoid PII exposure. Setting AZURE_TRACING_GEN_AI_CONTENT_RECORDING_ENABLED to true opts in to recording full prompt and response text.

questions.stem: An application calls a Model Router deployment and needs to log which underlying model handled each request for cost attribution. Where is that information available?

options.text: In a separate billing export only
In the response.model field of the API response
In the request headers sent by the client
It is never exposed to the caller

questions.explanation: The Model Router returns the name of the actual model it selected in the response.model field, which can be logged per turn for cost attribution and debugging.
